heading: Health care service demonstration projects in rural areas
label: 3032c Health care service demonstration projects in rural areas
notes: Amendments2006—Subsec. (a). , inserted “mental health services,” after “adult day health care,”.Pub. L. 109–365, § 404(1)Subsec. (b)(1)(B)(i). , inserted “mental health,” after “public health,”.Pub. L. 109–365, § 404(2)
amendments: Amendments2006—Subsec. (a). , inserted “mental health services,” after “adult day health care,”.Pub. L. 109–365, § 404(1)Subsec. (b)(1)(B)(i). , inserted “mental health,” after “public health,”.Pub. L. 109–365, § 404(2)

children.heading: Authority
children.label: a Authority
children.text: The Assistant Secretary, after consultation with the State agency of the State involved, shall make grants to eligible public agencies and nonprofit private organizations to pay part or all of the cost of developing or operating model health care service projects (including related home health care services, adult day health care, mental health services, outreach, and transportation) through multipurpose senior centers that are located in rural areas and that provide nutrition services under , to meet the health care needs of medically underserved older individuals residing in such areas.section 3030e of this title

children.label: A
children.text: experience in providing medical services of the type to be provided in the project for which a grant is requested; and

children.label: i
children.text: institutions of higher education having graduate programs with capability in public health, mental health, the medical sciences, psychology, pharmacology, nursing, social work, health education, nutrition, or gerontology, for the purpose of designing and developing such project; and

children.label: ii
children.text: critical access hospitals (as defined in and rural health clinics (as defined in );section 1395x(mm)(1) of this titlesection 1395x(aa)(2) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: in a rural area that has a population of less than 5,000; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in a county that has fewer than seven individuals per square mile; and

children.label: i
children.text: not less than 33⅓ of the population resides in rural areas; and

children.label: ii
children.text: not less than 5 percent of the population resides in counties with fewer than seven individuals per square mile,
assurances that the applicant will submit to the Assistant Secretary such evaluations and reports as the Assistant Secretary may require.

children.heading: Reports
children.label: c Reports
children.text: The Assistant Secretary shall prepare and submit to the appropriate committees of Congress a report that includes summaries of the evaluations and reports required under subsection (b).
